steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is a transition connecting the popular narrative (large-scale migration to California) with the study's finding (migrants 'merely relocated within the region'). The word 'merely' signals that local movement — not distant migration — was what actually happened, so the blank must introduce a contrasting, more typical pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘nevertheless,’: implies direct contradiction, but the study's finding doesn't flatly negate the narrative — it adds nuance about what was more common. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘additionally,’: implies the finding supplements or supports the narrative rather than complicates it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘for this reason,’: implies the regional relocation was caused by the narrative, which makes no logical sense. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘more often,’: marks regional relocation as the more typical outcome, directly contrasting with the California-migration narrative without overstating the opposition. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
